senses.definition: the status or title that a person gains by being the best player or team in a competition

examples.x: The team won the championship after a thrilling final match.
The championship was awarded to the player with the highest score.
Fans cheered as their favorite player secured the championship point.
The team’s goal is to defend their championship title next season.
The team won the championship after a thrilling final match.

senses.definition: a person or team finishing in second place in a competition or event

examples.x: She was disappointed to be the runner-up, but she was proud of how far she had come.
After a tough final, the player ended up as the runner-up in the championship.
The runner-up in the race still received a medal for their impressive performance.
He was happy to be the runner-up, even though he had hoped for first place.
The runner-up in the contest got a nice trophy as a reward.

senses.definition: a person who is good at sports and physical exercise, and often competes in sports competitions

examples.x: As a professional athlete, he maintained a strict diet and exercise routine.
As a well-rounded athlete, she competed in swimming, cycling, and running events.
Despite his age, the athlete continued to dominate in tennis.
The athlete's resilience and perseverance inspired many young sports enthusiasts.
The young athlete aspired to represent her country in the Olympics.

senses.definition: an athlete with a disability who competes in sports, often in events specifically designed for athletes with impairments

examples.x: She became a para athlete after a car accident left her with a spinal cord injury.
The para athlete overcame visual impairment to excel in judo at the Paralympic Games.
He was proud to represent his country as a para athlete in swimming.
Para athletes often demonstrate incredible strength and determination in their sports.

senses.definition: a person who holds a title or championship in a particular activity or sport
someone who competes against another person or group with the intention of winning, proving themselves, or achieving a specific goal

examples.x: She is the top challenger in the upcoming election.
The challenger in the debate presented compelling arguments.
The startup became a serious challenger to the industry giants.
The challenger in the race set a new personal best time.
The young boxer emerged as a strong challenger for the championship title.

senses.definition: the winner of a competition

examples.x: She was crowned champion after winning the tennis tournament.
The team celebrated their victory, finally becoming champions.
He trained for years to become the champion of the boxing league.
The reigning champion defended his title successfully.
She proudly held up the trophy as the new champion.

senses.definition: a participant who has reached the final stage or round of a competition

examples.x: The finalist prepared diligently for the championship match.
During the tournament, the finalist demonstrated exceptional skill and determination.
Every finalist competes with the goal of winning the title or championship.
The finalist adjusted their strategy to adapt to the high-pressure environment.
She focused on maintaining her peak physical and mental condition as a finalist.

senses.definition: someone who competes with others in a sport event

examples.x: The marathon attracted thousands of competitors from around the world.
She trained for years to become a top competitor in the Olympic Games.
Each competitor received a number and instructions before the race began.
The young competitor impressed everyone with his skills on the basketball court.
As the oldest competitor in the tournament, he inspired many with his perseverance.

senses.definition: a player known for effectively completing scoring opportunities

examples.x: The team relies on their star finisher to score crucial goals.
As a reliable finisher, she rarely misses a shot on goal.
The coach praised the young player's development as a finisher.
He earned the reputation of being the league's top finisher last season.
The finisher's precision in front of the net is unmatched.

senses.definition: a participant, team, or horse that is considered unlikely to win or achieve success, often due to lower ranking, lesser experience, or underdog status

examples.x: The outsider surprised everyone by advancing to the semifinals of the tournament.
She focused on improving her skills and mental toughness to overcome the outsider label.
The outsider's performance caught the attention of fans and pundits alike.
The outsider appreciated the support of their supporters who believed in their potential.
The coach provided guidance on exploiting opportunities and staying focused as an outsider in the competition.

senses.definition: someone who has won a medal in a competition, typically finishing in one of the top three positions

examples.x: Every medalist strives for excellence and dedication to achieve success in their sport.
As a talented pianist, he became a medalist in multiple international music competitions.
At the awards ceremony, the medalist proudly stood on the podium to receive their medal.
The author's novel received critical acclaim and made her a medalist in literary circles.
He was honored as a medalist in humanitarian efforts for his dedicated work in disaster relief.

senses.definition: someone who takes part in the Paralympic games
a person who fails to win or succeed in a competition, contest, etc.

examples.x: Although he was an also-ran in the race, he kept pushing himself to improve for the next competition.
The team was considered an also-ran before the tournament, but they surprised everyone with their performance.
The underdog team was seen as an also-ran by many, but they managed to win the championship.
He was just an also-ran in the game, finishing far behind the leaders, but he still gave it his best effort.

senses.definition: someone who is usually older or the most skilled in a sport, often competing in a higher age group or against the best opponents
a person who wins third place in a competition or event

examples.x: The bronze medalist celebrated her victory with her team.
As a bronze medalist, he proudly stood on the podium.
The young gymnast was thrilled to become a bronze medalist.
She trained hard to achieve her dream of being a bronze medalist.
The bronze medalist waved to the cheering crowd.

senses.definition: a person who has won a gold medal in a competition

examples.x: The event concluded with the crowning of the gold medalist.
Every athlete dreams of becoming a gold medalist.
Being a gold medalist requires immense dedication.
Our team’s gold medalist inspired many young athletes.
He is a three-time gold medalist in swimming.

senses.definition: an athlete who finishes in second place in a competition

examples.x: Being a silver medalist motivated him to strive for gold in the next championship.
She framed the certificate declaring her as a silver medalist and hung it on her bedroom wall.
The silver medalist's family cheered loudly from the stands as the results were announced.
The team's captain was proud to be a silver medalist after a hard-fought tournament.
The silver medalist celebrated on the podium with a wide smile.

senses.definition: a person that currently holds the best or highest achievement in a particular field or category

examples.x: She is the record holder for the fastest mile in her age group.
The athlete broke the record and became the new record holder in the discus throw.
The team is the record holder for the most wins in a single season.
She set a new record in sales and became the record holder for that quarter.
He became the new record holder for the highest number of goals scored in a season.

senses.definition: a participant who has advanced to the semi-final round of a competition or tournament

examples.x: The soccer team celebrated their victory, knowing they were now semi-finalists.
The judges selected ten semi-finalists from hundreds of audition tapes.
After a tense match, they emerged as semi-finalists in the tennis championship.
Her artwork impressed the jury, securing her place as a semi-finalist in the art competition.
Being a semi-finalist in the cooking contest meant she was one step closer to the prize.

senses.definition: a competitor who withdraws from a race or competition before it starts

examples.x: Due to an injury, the runner was a scratch from the marathon.
Just before the event, the cyclist became a scratch due to illness.
The swimmer was listed as a scratch in the final heat.
The team announced their star player was a scratch for tonight's game.
An unexpected scratch left the team scrambling for a replacement.
